Keep faculty-specific assessment claims explicitly scoped to the Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences.
Do not infer binding university-wide student assessment rules from faculty pages or source-index pages.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found University of Zurich central AI guiding principles, UZH generative-AI recommendations for teaching and assessment, the UZH.ai policy index, and a Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences assessment page. Claims are narrow and separate university-wide central guidance from faculty-specific assessment rules.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommendations on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence at UZH

artifacts.claimText: The University of Zurich has seven guiding principles on the use of artificial intelligence in research and teaching, adopted by the Extended Executive Board on 26 September 2023 and later expanded and supplemented.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are shaping and changing the way people conduct research and teach. Considering the risks and opportunities, the Extended Executive Board of the University adopted the following guiding principles governing the use of this cutting-edge technology on 26 September 2023, which have since been expanded and supplemented.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UZH states that its Extended Executive Board adopted seven guiding principles for AI use in research and teaching on 26 September 2023, later expanded and supplemented.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Seven Guiding Principles section, introductory paragraph
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged claim directly supported by an official University of Zurich source. This is not marked canonical or human-reviewed.

artifacts.claimText: UZH guiding principles say members are transparent about AI-tool use, users bear sole responsibility for AI-assisted content and products, and AI tools must be used in accordance with statutory provisions including data protection and copyright law.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: UZH members are transparent about their use of AI tools. The sole responsibility for content and products generated with the help of AI lies with the relevant users. UZH members use AI tools in accordance with the statutory provisions (in particular, data protection and copyright law).
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The UZH principles require transparency about AI use, user responsibility for AI-assisted content, and compliance with statutory provisions including data protection and copyright law.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged claim directly supported by an official University of Zurich source. This is not marked canonical or human-reviewed.

artifacts.claimText: UZH guiding principles say faculties should ensure equal opportunities and fair assessment conditions whether AI tools are permitted or prohibited, and that UZH will uphold academic integrity and penalize violations of its disciplinary or integrity rules.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The faculties ensure equal opportunities and fair conditions when carrying out assessments regardless of whether the use of AI tools as aids is permitted or prohibited. UZH will uphold academic integrity in all its activities and penalise violations of its Disciplinary Regulations and/or its Integrity Ordinance.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The UZH principles require fair assessment conditions whether AI aids are permitted or prohibited and say UZH penalizes violations of disciplinary or integrity rules.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged claim directly supported by an official University of Zurich source. This is not marked canonical or human-reviewed.

artifacts.claimText: UZH recommendations say faculties and study program directors are responsible for specific generative-AI guidelines and should ensure students know whether and how generative AI may be used in assessments.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommendations on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence at UZH
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The faculties and the individual study program directors are responsible for drawing up specific guidelines and policies. Those responsible must ensure that students are aware of whether and to what extent generative AI may be used in assessments. If the use of generative AI is not or only partially permitted, they should ensure the relevant assessments are conducted fairly.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UZH recommendations place responsibility on faculties and study program directors to set specific guidelines and ensure students know whether and how generative AI may be used in assessments.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Use of artificial intelligence in Teaching, Responsibilities
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UZH recommendations say a declaration of authenticity can address generative-AI tools, that the person completing it should confirm only permitted aids or tools were used, and that any generative-AI use must always be indicated.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recommendations on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence at UZH
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: A declaration of authenticity can be used for written assessments to confirm, among other things, that the tools and sources used have been correctly cited and referenced. The person completing the declaration must confirm that they have only used the permitted aids or tools. Any use of generative AI tools must always be indicated.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UZH recommendations say declarations of authenticity can address generative AI, require confirmation that only permitted tools were used, and state that any generative-AI use must always be indicated.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Declaration of authenticity when using generative AI tools
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For the University of Zurich Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences, teaching staff determine for each module the extent to which AI is permitted for assessments; use of tools or generative AI without explicit consent and disclosure is punished as academic misconduct.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences is generally supports the productive and considered use of AI. Teaching staff determine for each module the extent to which the use of AI is permitted for assessments. The use of AI can also be completely excluded. The use of tools or generative AI without the explicit consent of the teaching staff and without disclosing the tools will be punished as academic misconduct.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For the Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences, teaching staff determine per module whether AI is permitted, and undisclosed or unauthorized use is punished as academic misconduct.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UZH.ai presents a current collection of central and faculty-specific AI guidelines and says UZH is working on a unified university-wide AI policy.

citation.citationTitle: University of Zurich AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Below you’ll find the current collection of central and faculty-specific guidelines. This list will grow as more faculties publish their own frameworks. That means: each faculty is free to define policies that fit its teaching and research needs — while we’re also working behind the scenes on a unified, university-wide AI policy.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The UZH.ai policy hub describes current central and faculty-specific guidelines and says a unified university-wide AI policy is being worked on.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: UZH AI Policies introduction and policy table

artifacts.title: University of Zurich AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for the University of Zurich found central UZH AI guiding principles, UZH recommendations on generative AI in teaching and assessment, the UZH.ai policy hub, and a Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences assessment page. Seven agent-reviewed claim candidates were kept narrow and separated university-wide principles from faculty-specific assessment rules.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Only public official UZH pages were used; no login-gated, private, CAPTCHA-protected, or paywalled sources were accessed.
Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences assessment claims are scoped to that faculty and are not presented as university-wide student assessment rules.
The UZH.ai policy hub status claim is treated as a source-status note, not as a binding policy rule.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
